slides.title: Different Inputs, Same Pattern 🧪
slides.content:
# Textarea, Select, and Checkbox

React handles all form elements uniformly with `value` and `onChange`.

## Textarea

In HTML, `<textarea>` uses its children as value. In React, it uses a `value` prop.

```jsx
<textarea value={text} onChange={handleChange} />
```

## Select

In HTML, the selected option is set with `selected`. In React, you set `value` on the `<select>` itself.

```jsx
<select value={selected} onChange={handleChange}>
  <option value="a">A</option>
  <option value="b">B</option>
</select>
```

## Checkbox

Checkboxes use `checked` instead of `value`.

```jsx
<input type="checkbox" checked={isChecked} onChange={e => setIsChecked(e.target.checked)} />
```

slides.title: Examples

quizData.question: In React, you set the selected option of a dropdown by adding `selected` to the `<option>` tag.
quizData.explanation: False. You set `value` on the `<select>` element, and React matches it to the option's value.
